classification.title: An Act to repeal, amend and re-enact Section 2 of Chapter 28 of the Acts of 1918, being Section 2223b-2, Kentucky Statutes, Carroll's Edition, 1930, relating to the organization of loan and investment companies in cities of the first and second classes and extending the terms thereof to cities of the fifth class. Fixing the number of persons who may associate themselves together to establish a loan
classification.summary: This act amends existing statutes concerning the organization of loan and investment companies. It specifically repeals, amends, and re-enacts Section 2 of Chapter 28 of the Acts of 1918, which is codified as Section 2223b-2 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes (1930 Edition). The amendment extends the provisions for organizing these companies from cities of the first and second classes to also include cities of the fifth class. It also fixes the minimum number of persons required to establish such a company.

Turner, William Cox, Floyd Carnes, Malinda Saylor) to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission. Chapter 425 designates the federal highway connecting Mammoth Cave National Park and the Great Smoky Mountains National Park as the Cordell Hull Parkway. Chapter 428 provides for the official publication of legislative digests.
classification.reasoning: The provided text consists of a table of contents for a legislative session. The entries describe resolutions authorizing individuals to sue the state and highway commission, designating a national parkway, and establishing official publications. None of these resolutions contain any language or provisions that suggest racial discrimination or segregation, which are characteristic of Jim Crow laws. Therefore, the statute is classified as 'no' for Jim Crow, with high confidence. The category is null as it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as it is a listing of acts from a legislative session. The racial indicator is 'none' as race is not mentioned or implied.

OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE
SEVENTH , AND ENDED ON SATURDAY , FEBRUARY THE
FIFTEENTH , NINETEEN HUNDRED THIRTY -SIX .
CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT providing the means whereby the sense of the people
of any county , city , town, district or precinct may be taken
as to whether spirituous , vinous or malt liquors shall be sold ,
bartered or loaned therein ; providing for committees of in-
terested parties and their representation at the election ; pro-
viding a means for contesting said election ; prohibiting the
sale , loaning, furnishing or giving of intoxicating liquors on
the day of election ; defining spirituous , vinous or malt liquors
and other terms ; prohibiting the sale , barter , loan procure-
ment and giving of such liquors , and the possession and
transportation thereof for said purposes , in territory voting
therefor, excepting sacramental wine and certain other alco-
holic liquors and uses , and sales by distillers and manufac-
turers ; declaring certain acts and conditions as nuisances
and providing for the abatement of same and forfeitures of
property in certain instances ; providng for searches and
seizures ; declaring the duties of courts , judges and other
officers ; conferring jurisdiction upon inferior courts concur-
rent with circuit courts ; and providing for enforcement of the
Act and penalties for its violation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
DIVISION ONE (I)
Section One (1 ) . Definitions . As used in this
Act the following terms , unless the text otherwise

classification.title: AN ACT providing the means whereby the sense of the people of any county, city, town, district or precinct may be taken as to whether spirituous, vinous or malt liquors shall be sold, bartered or loaned therein
classification.summary: This act outlines the process for local option elections regarding the sale of alcoholic beverages. It details how the public's opinion can be surveyed on whether to permit the sale, barter, or loan of spirituous, vinous, or malt liquors within a specific geographic area. The statute also covers election procedures, contestation, prohibitions on liquor sales on election days, definitions of liquors, restrictions on possession and transportation, and enforcement mechanisms including search, seizure, and penalties. It also addresses nuisances and forfeitures related to violations.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns local option elections for the sale of alcoholic beverages. It does not contain any explicit or implicit references to race, nor does it establish any discriminatory practices based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as it is an act passed by the General Assembly during a specific session. The confidence is high because the subject matter is clearly defined and unrelated to racial segregation or discrimination.

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
indicates , shall have the following meanings respec-
tively .
(a) " Local option territory " or " territory "
means such county , city , town , district or precinct
for which a petition shall be filed asking for an
election to take the sense of the voters regarding the
sale , barter and loan of intoxicating liquors as pro-
vided , and such governmental unit where a majority
shall have voted in favor of prohibiting the sale ,
barter or loan of said liquors or the possession or
transportation thereof .
(b) " Spirituous , vinous or malt liquors " means ,
respectively , intoxicating liquor . " Intoxicating
liquor " means alcoholic brandy , whiskey , rum , gin ,
beer , ale , porter and wine and in addition thereto any
spirituous , vinous , malt or fermented liquors , liquids
and compounds , whether medicated , proprietary ,
patented or not , and by whatever name called con-
taining one per centum or more of alcohol by volume
which are fit for use for beverage purposes .
(c) The word " person " includes the singular
and plural number , and shall mean and include a
natural person , association , co -partnership or cor-
poration .
(d ) " Election " means an election to be held for
the purpose of taking the sense of the people as to
the prohibition or permission of the sale , barter or
loan of spirituous , vinous or malt liquors in any
territory .
(e) " Prohibition " means that the sale , barter
or loan of intoxicating liquors have been rendered.
unlawful under the terms of this Act .
(f) " Local option law " refers to the provisions
of this Act .
DIVISION TWO (II )
Section Two ( 2 ) . Petition for election . Upon ap-
plication by written petition filed with the clerk of

CHAPTER 1 3
the county court of any county signed by a number
of legal voters in said county , or any city , town ,
district or precinct thereof to be affected , equal to
twenty-five ( 25 ) per cent of the votes cast in said
territory at the last preceding general election , it
shall be the duty of the judge of the county court of
such county at the current or the next regular term
of said court thereafter to make an order on the
order book of said court directing an election to be
held in such county , city , town , district or precinct
to be affected thereby , for the purpose of taking the
sense of the legal voters of such county , city , town ,
district or precinct upon the proposition whether
or not spirituous , vinous or malt liquors shall be
sold , bartered or loaned therein .
Section Three ( 3 ) . Subscription to petition .
Said petition , in addition to the subscription of the
name of the voter , shall state his postoffice address
and the correct date upon which same was signed .
No such subscriber shall be permitted to withdraw
his name or have it taken from such petition after
same shall have been filed as aforesaid . Provided ,
that if the name of any person shall have been sub-
scribed to such petition without his authority , he
may appear before the county judge before said
election shall be ordered held and upon proof to
that effect his name may be eliminated by an order
of the court and when so eliminated , the same shall
not be counted as a petitioner . Said petition may
consist of one or more separate units .
Section Four (4 ) . Day of election . The day upon
which the election shall be held may be stated in the
petition . If it is not so stated , it shall be designated
by the county judge ; provided
(a) The election shall not be earlier than sixty
or later than ninety days after the date said appli-
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1936, p. 78

cation is lodged in the office of the clerk of the county

(b) The election shall not be held on the same
day that a primary or general election is held in the
territory or any part thereof , nor within thirty days
next preceding or following a regular political elec-
tion .
( c ) No election in any territory less than the
county shall be held on the same day on which an
election for the entire county is held .
(d ) No election shall be held in the same terri-
tory oftener than once in every three years .
Section Five ( 5 ) . Notice of election . Within
five days after said order of the county court calling
an election is made , it shall be the duty of the county
clerk to give to the sheriff of the county a certified
copy of the same. It shall then be the duty of the
said sheriff to have same published in some weekly
or daily newspaper published in the county for at
least two weeks before the election , and also to ad-
vertise same by written or printed handbills posted
at not less than five conspicuous places in each pre-
cinct of the county for the same length of time when
the election is held for the entire county ; and when
the election is ordered held in a city , town , district
or precinct , then at five conspicuous places therein
for the same length of time . In case there is no daily
or weekly newspaper published in the county , or the
proprietor of such newspaper refuses to publish
said advertisement , the printed or written handbills
posted as herein provided for shall be sufficient . It
shall be the duty of the sheriff to report in writing
to the county court that the notices have been duly
published and posted .
Section Six ( 6 ) . Form of ballot . The proposi-
tion to be voted upon shall be stated on the ballot

Section Fourteen ( 14 ) . Sale on election day pro-
hibited . Upon each day that an election is held
under the provisions of this Act , all saloons and
other places where intoxicating liquors are sold by

be kept closed during the whole of said day . It shall
be unlawful during said day for any person in said

any intoxicating liquor except in such cases as are
excluded from the provisions of this Act . Any per-
son found guilty under the provisions of this section
shall be subject to the penalties prescribed in Sec-
tion twenty -eight ( 28 ) of this Act .
Section Fifteen ( 15 ) . Effective date of local op-

be in favor of prohibiting the sale of intoxicating

have been held , the law prohibiting the same shall
be in force and effect at the expiration of sixty days
from the date of the entry of the certificate of the

court . After the expiration of said period , no license

thereof as the unexpired period of the license bears .

classification.title: An Act relating to intoxicating liquors in local option territory
classification.summary: This act makes it unlawful to solicit or receive orders for intoxicating liquors, or to advertise their sale in local option territory. It also prohibits the transportation, transfer, delivery, or distribution of intoxicating liquor into local option territory, and makes it unlawful for any person in such territory to receive or accept intoxicating liquor from a common carrier or other person who transported it for compensation, hire, or profit. Exceptions are made for distillers, manufacturers, authorized agents, and wholesale dealers making contracts for barrel, case, or package lots to be shipped into lawful sale territories.
classification.reasoning: The statute addresses the regulation of intoxicating liquors in 'local option territory.' It prohibits various activities related to the sale, advertising, and transportation of alcohol within these designated areas. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly target or discriminate against any racial group. The focus is solely on the prohibition and regulation of alcohol sales and distribution based on local option status. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

Section Thirty -Five ( 35 ) . Indictment ; allega-

indictment charging the commission of an offense
under this Act that a vote was taken or an election
held , or any other step relative thereto , in the terri-
tory where said offense is charged to have been com-
mitted , but it may be simply stated that the act or

upon defendant . No person shall possess , or have

shall have been lawfully acquired and is intended
to be used lawfully ; and in any suit, prosecution ,
proceeding or action or motion , the burden of prov-
ing that same was lawfully acquired and is intended
to be used lawfully shall be upon the defendant or
defendants .

cution or proceeding for any violation of this Act ,
the general reputation of the defendant for boot-
legging or being engaged in selling , or trading in ,
or keeping for sale , or transporting for sale , shall
be admissible in evidence against said defendant or
defendants .

Section Forty - One ( 41 ) . Distillers and manufac-

any section , paragraph , or part of this Act shall be
held to be unconstitutional , the remainder of this
Act shall continue effective and enforceable notwith-
standing such decision .
An emergency is hereby declared to exist , and
this Act shall be effective from and after its passage
and approval by the Governor .

CHAPTER 3 .
AN ACT appropriating the sum of seventy -five ( $75.00 ) dollars
per month for the use of the widow and children of Powell
Hayes , deceased .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Whereas , on the twenty-first
(21st ) day of December , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Powell Hayes while serving as a
State Patrolman in the discharge of his duty, at-
tempted to arrest two men suspected of having vio-
lated the laws of this Commonwealth and the Fed- .
eral government , in the city of Franklin , Simpson
County, Kentucky , was shot by said suspects , from
which gunshot wound he died on the twenty-first
(21st ) day of December , and whereas , he left surviv-
ing him a widow and three infant children , leaving
said widow and children in destitute circumstances :
Section Two ( 2 ) . It is hereby appropriated out
of the general expenditure fund for the use and
benefit of the widow of Powell Hayes , deceased
(Mrs. Nell Hayes ) and children of said decedent the
sum of seventy -five ( $ 75.00 ) dollars per month , pro-
viding that payments stop at the twenty-fourth
monthly payment .

classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Statute 596 (1930 Edition) which was originally enacted in 1893 and amended in 1916. The amendment changes the requirement for banks and trust companies regarding the distribution of profits and dividends. Specifically, it alters the condition under which dividends can be paid to common stockholders, requiring that preferred stock dividends must be paid first, or that a sum equal to the common capital stock must be set aside before common stock dividends are declared. The stated purpose is to increase the surplus of these financial institutions.

It also outlines powers related to accepting drafts and issuing letters of credit.

CHAPTER 15 .
AN ACT relating to the release of prisoners from penitentiaries ,
state reformatories and houses of reform in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and providing for notice to county officials
and district attorneys , when said persons are released from
any of said institutions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . It shall be the duty of the warden
of the penitentiary , the superintendent of the State
reformatory and the superintendent of the houses
of reform in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , upon
the release of any prisoner or inmate from confine-
ment in any of said institutions to immediately
notify the Commonwealth's Attorney of the dis-
trict , the county attorney and sheriff of the county
and the chief of police of the city , to which the said
inmate is released , of said release and giving his
residence and the person's name to whom he was
released .
Section Two . All laws or part of laws in con-
flict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 5 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT relating to prisoners now or hereafter confined in
State prisons ; providing for the working of such prisoners
on prison farms , highways , and other public works ; providing
for the care , supervision , disciplining , maintenance , trans-
portation , and housing of such prisoners while so engaged
and for the payment of the costs and expenses thereof ;
authorizing payment of compensation to such prisoners and
their dependent families ; repealing all laws and parts of
laws in conflict with the Act ; and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . The State Welfare Board is hereby
authorized and directed to classify for working pur-
poses all prisoners now or hereafter confined in
the State prisons . In making such classifications
the State Welfare Board shall take into account the
past record of each prisoner before and after con-
finement in prison , his age, physical fitness for vari-
ous types of work and such other conditions as the
Board may deem proper in order to classify each
and every prisoner as to his general fitness and
suitability for work within and without the prison.
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1936, p. 126

hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , by a truck , sustaining the fracture
of the bones of the leg between the knee and ankle and
mashed in the hip and from which he was caused to suffer

classification.title: An Act to Amend and Re-enact Section 59 of Chapter 21
classification.summary: This statute amends a previous act concerning the approval of bonds and obligations issued by counties, cities, municipalities, or other taxing districts. It requires that before a court approves such a bond, the issuing entity must prove that the indebtedness was created within constitutional limits. The entity must also detail all existing and unpaid debts during the period the indebtedness was created. If the court finds that officials did not manage finances responsibly during the creation of the debt, the bond issue will not be approved. The burden of proof lies with the taxing district. A provision is made for refunding indebtedness evidenced by a judgment.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One ( 1 ) . The Governor , by a request in writing ,
or either house of the General Assembly by a reso-
lution of such house , may obtain the written opinion
of the justices of the Court of Appeals of Kentucky ,
or a majority thereof, on important constitutional
questions , and all such opinions shall be published
in volumes entitled " Advisory Opinions of the Court
of Appeals .
Two ( 2 ) . The General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky being now in regular ses-
sion and many important measures being intro-
duced at its daily sessions , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect from
and after its passage and approval by the Governor .

Equity Jurisdiction ; Cause for Divorce .-A jury
shall not be impaneled in any action for divorce ,
alimony or maintenance , but courts having general
equity jurisdiction may grant a divorce for any of
the following causes , to both husband and wife :
One ( 1 ) . To both parties . Such impotency or
malformation as prevents sexual intercourse .
Two ( 2 ) . Living apart without any cohabitation
for five consecutive years next before the applica-
tion .
To party not in fault . Also to the party not in
fault , for the following causes :
One ( 1 ) . Abandonment by one party or the other
for one year .
Two ( 2 ) . Living in adultery with another man
or woman .
Three ( 3 ) . Condemnation for felony in or out
of this State .
Four (4 ) . Concealment from the other party of
any loathsome disease existing at the time of mar-
riage , or contracting such afterward .
Five ( 5 ) . Force , duress or fraud in obtaining
the marriage.
Six ( 6 ) . Uniting with any religious society whose
creed and rules require a renunciation of the mar-
riage covenant , or forbid husband and wife from
cohabiting .
To the wife . Also to the wife , when not in like
fault , for the following causes :
One ( 1 ) . Confirmed habit of drunkenness on the
part of the husband of not less than one year's
durating accompanied with a wasting of his estate ,
and without any suitable provision for the main-
tenance of his wife or children .

classification.summary: This act outlines the procedures for public notice and hearings regarding the refunding of bonded indebtedness for drainage districts in Kentucky. classification.summary: This act repeals and re-enacts sections of the 1934 Kentucky Statutes concerning retirement systems for teachers and other school employees in independent school districts embracing cities of the first, second, and third classes. It empowers the Boards of Education of these districts to establish such systems, granting them corporate powers and the name 'The Teachers Retirement System of the Schools of Kentucky'. It also allows districts already operating insurance and annuity systems to adopt, modify, or change them.

classification.title: An Act to amend Section 3 of Article 5 of Chapter 65 of the Acts of the General Assembly of nineteen thirty-four (1934), being Section 4399-3, Kentucky Statutes, relating to independent school districts.
classification.summary: This statute amends existing law regarding independent school districts. It specifies that independent school districts embracing cities of the first five classes, along with their surrounding territories, and any independent graded common school districts with a census of 250 or more white children, will be designated as independent school districts. It also sets a condition for continued operation: districts (other than those in cities of the first five classes already operating as independent districts) must maintain a census of at least 250 white children, unless the State Board of Education determines that operating as an independent district allows for a more efficient school service program.
classification.reasoning: The statute explicitly mentions 'white children' in its criteria for defining and maintaining independent school districts. This explicit racial classification in educational policy is a hallmark of Jim Crow-era legislation, which often created separate or unequal educational opportunities based on race. Therefore, it is classified as a Jim Crow law. The confidence is high because of the explicit mention of race. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and the amendment of a previous act. The category is 'education' as the statute directly concerns school districts.

classification.title: An Act to provide for the education of certain citizens of the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
classification.summary: This act provides for the payment of tuition and fees by the Commonwealth of Kentucky for residents who are qualified to study at the University of Kentucky or other state institutions but cannot due to Section 187 of the Kentucky Constitution. These individuals are permitted to pursue such courses at educational institutions outside of the state, and their expenses will be covered. The State Superintendent of Public Instruction will ascertain and pay these tuition and fees, with the State Board of Education prescribing rules and regulations. Funds are appropriated for this purpose, with a limit of $175.00 per person per school year.
keywords: Section 187 of the Constitution of Kentucky
classification.reasoning: The statute explicitly references 'Section one hundred eighty-seven (187) of the Constitution of Kentucky' as a barrier preventing certain individuals from pursuing courses at the University of Kentucky or other state institutions. Historical context reveals that Section 187 of the Kentucky Constitution, as amended in 1897, mandated separate educational facilities for white and Black students. This statute, by providing state funding for Black students to attend out-of-state institutions when they were barred from in-state ones due to this segregationist constitutional provision, implicitly acknowledges and perpetuates racial segregation in education. Therefore, it is classified as a Jim Crow law.

means or method of nominating candidates for any
elective office other than by primary elections as
provided in this Act .

It outlines the process for qualified electors to have their names printed on the party's primary ballot by filing a notification and declaration. It also allows election commissioners in counties with first, second, and third-class cities to employ extra tabulators to expedite ballot counting.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the process of primary elections and the nomination of candidates. It does not contain any explicit or implicit racial classifications or restrictions. The mention of election commissioners and tabulators in specific city classes is a procedural detail related to election administration and does not indicate a Jim Crow law. Therefore, it is classified as 'no' for Jim Crow, with a high confidence. The category is 'voting' as it directly relates to election processes.

classification.title: An Act to amend and re-enact Section 4706 of the Kentucky Statutes, Carroll's Edition 1930, as amended by Chapter 91 of the Acts of 1932, relating to the investment of funds by fiduciaries.
classification.summary: This statute amends existing law to permit fiduciaries to invest funds in a wider range of government and private securities, including those issued by the U.S. Government, the State of Kentucky (including bridge revenue bonds), federal farm loan acts, Home Owners Loan Corporation, Federal Farm Mortgage Corporation, Federal Intermediate Credit Bank, Federal Housing Administrator, and safe real estate mortgage notes. It also allows fiduciaries to charge borrowers up to 2% of the principal for real estate mortgage loans to cover costs and services.

That Chapter eighty -three ( 83 ) of the Acts of the

No person shall , in the state of Kentucky , hunt ,
pursue , chase, catch , kill , injure or molest any elk
or deer . Any person violating this section , or any
feature thereof , shall upon conviction , be fined not
less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) or more than one
hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) for each offense .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 56 .
AN ACT to create a Game and Fish Commission , to define its
powers and duties , and to provide for the protection , restora-
tion , propagation , and increase of game , birds , fish and fur-
bearing animals ; and transferring duties and funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
GAME AND FISH COMMISSION
Section One ( 1 ) . ( a ) Commission Created . That
the Game and Fish Department is hereby placed
under a Commission to be known as the " Game and
Fish Commission " , hereinafter referred to as the
Commission , which is hereby created .
(b) Appointment and Terms . The Commission
shall consist of five members , who shall fairly repre-
sent the several geographical sections of the State
(and not more than three of whom shall be of the
same political party ) .

(f) Office and Supplies . The Commission shall
have its principal office in the State Capital or such
other place as may be designated in this Act . The
Commission is authorized to purchase all supplies ,
equipment , printed forms , and notices , and to issue
such publications as it may deem necessary to carry
out the purposes of this Act .
GENERAL POWERS AND DUTIES OF THE COMMISSION
Section Two ( 2 ) . ( a) Chairman and Meeting .
The members of the Commission shall meet at the
Capital within thirty days after their appointment
and shall organize by electing from their member-
ship a Chairman and a Secretary, and thereafter
regular meetings shall be held by the Commission
at its offices quarterly , and at such other times and
places within the State as the Commission shall se-
lect for the transaction of business .
(b) Powers and Duties . It shall be unlawful to
take or pursue any of the wildlife of the State at any
time or in any manner , except at such times and in
such manner as the supply of said wildlife may
justify , and the said Commission is hereby directed
to make adequate investigation as to the said supply
and thereupon shall , by appropriate rules and regu-
lations :
-One (1 ) Establish and close to hunting , trapping ,
or fishing, game , bird or fish refuges on public lands
and , with the consent of the owner , on private lands ;
Two (2 ) -Acquire by purchase , condemnation ,
lease , agreement , gift, or devise , lands or waters
suitable for the purposes hereinafter enumerated ,
and develop , operate and maintain the same for said
purposes :

CHAPTER 56 163
necessary for the conduct of the work of the Com-

The Commission shall authorize such scientific and
other studies as may be deemed necessary to its
work , and shall collect , classify , and disseminate such ·
statistics , data , and information as in its discretion
will tend to promote the objects of this Act .
OFFICE OF DIRECTOR
Secion Three ( 3 ) . ( a ) Director to be Selected .
The office of Director of the Game and Fish Depart-
ment is hereby created . Upon the organization of
the Commission hereinbefore provided for , said
Commission shall appoint a Director of the Game
and Fish Department , who shall be a man with
knowledge of , and experience in , the requirements
for the protection , conservation , and restoration of
the wildlife resources of the State . The Director
shall serve for an indefinite term . The Director
shall not hold any other public office and shall devote
his entire time to the service of the State in the dis-
charge of his official duties .
(b) Compensation and Expenses . The Director
shall receive such compensation as the Commission
may determine , and shall be reimbursed for all
actual and necessary traveling and other expenses
incurred by him in the discharge of his official duties .
(c) Oath and Bond . Before entering upon the
duties of his office , the Director shall take , and sub-
scribe to , the constitutional oath of office and shall ,
in addition thereto , swear or affirm that he holds
no other public office , nor any position under any
political committee or party . Such oath , or affirma-
tion , shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of
State .

CHAPTER 57 .
AN ACT for the protection of Wild Turkey , Imported Pheasant
and Hungarian Patridge , amending and re -enacting Section
one thousand nine hundred thirty -eight a-six ( 1938a -6 ) , Ken-
tucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter eighty -two ( 82 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being Section one thousand nine
hundred thirty -eight a- 6 ( 1938a - 6 ) , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) Supplement , be amended and re-
enacted , and when so amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows , to -wit :
No person shall catch , kill or pursue with such in-
tent , or have the same in his possession after it has
been caught or killed , any wild turkey, imported
pheasant and Hungarian partridge . Any person
violating this section , or any feature thereof, shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor and shall , upon convic-
tion be fined not less than fifteen dollars ($15.00 )
nor more than fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) for each offense .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

damage .

classification.title: An Act Relating to Road Houses and Public Nuisances
classification.summary: This act grants concurrent jurisdiction to Quarterly and Circuit Courts to try charges related to road houses operating as public nuisances. It mandates peace officers and county patrolmen to regularly visit such premises and make arrests without warrants for violations observed in their presence. The act exempts private homes and the entertainment of bona fide guests therein. It also clarifies that the act does not apply to filling stations, grocery stores, general stores, or other businesses not conducted for entertainment purposes. The act declares an emergency and becomes effective upon adoption and approval.

It outlines enforcement procedures for peace officers and county patrolmen, and establishes court jurisdiction for related charges. Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One 1. That Chapter 19 of the Acts
of the Extraordinary Session of the General As-
sembly of 1934 be repealed , amended and reenact-
ed so that when said Act is reenacted it shall read
as follows :
Automobiles primarily designed for carrying pas-
sengers and having provisions for not more than
seven passengers , including the operator , shall be
classed as passenger automobiles and the annual
registration fees for same shall be as follows :
For each passenger car having less than 22 horse-
power, Six Dollars ( $ 6.00 ) ; for each such car hav-
ing 22 horsepower and less than 32 horsepower ,
Seven Dollars and twenty cents ( $7.20 ) ; for each
such car having 32 horsepower or more , Twelve
Dollars ($ 12.00 ) , and " horsepower " within the
meaning of this Act shall be calculated on the rating
fixed by the Society of Automobile Engineers .
Section Two 2. The registration fee for each
motorcycle shall be Three Dollars ($ 3.00 ) , and for
each sidecar attached thereto , One Dollar and Fifty
Cents ($1.50 ) .
Section Three 3. Whereas the people of Ken-
tucky have expressed themselves as predomi-
nately in favor of the repeal of the " gross receipts
Act of 1934 " and whereas Chapter 19 of the Acts
of the Extraordinary Session of the General As-
sembly of 1934 was enacted by reason of the enact-
ment of the said gross receipts Act of 1934 and
whereas said gross receipts Act and Chapter 19 of
the Acts of the Extraordinary Session of 1934 con-
stitutes an unfair and unreasonable burden on cer-
tain classes of taxpayers, an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective

The act declares an emergency due to public favor for repealing the 'gross receipts Act of 1934', which was the basis for the original Chapter 19, and states that both acts imposed an unfair burden on certain taxpayers.

laws in conflict herewith and repealing so much of the Act
of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty -six (1936 ) heretofore enacted as fixes the amount of
said registration fees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Automobiles primarily designed
for carrying passengers and having provisions for
not more than seven (7 ) passengers including the
operator , shall be classed as passenger automobiles ,
and the annual registration fee for the same shall
be four dollars and fifty cents ($4.50 ) per year, and
the County Clerk who issues said registration fee
shall receive the fee now allowed by law .
Section Two . The registration fee for each
motorcycle shall be two dollars ( $ 2.00 ) per year
and for each side car attachment thereto , one dol-
lar ($1.00 ) per year.
Section Three . All laws or parts of laws in
conflict herewith are hereby repealed and so much
of the Act of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty- six (1936 ) heretofore
adopted , which fixes the amount of the registration
fees of said automobiles or motorcycles or sidecars
is hereby repealed .
Section Four . This law shall not become ef-
fective until January first ( 1st ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty - seven ( 1937 ) .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .

classification.title: An Act Relating to Motor Vehicle Registration and Financial Responsibility
classification.summary: This act outlines provisions for suspending an owner's certificate of registration if they fail to provide proof of ability to respond in damages. It prohibits the transfer of registration to defeat the act's purpose and allows for such transfers upon furnishing proof of financial responsibility by the transferee. Section Fifteen . Any person whose operator's

pended or revoked and restoration thereof or is-

ceeding six ( 6 ) months or by both such fine and im-
prisonment .
RESTRICTIONS IN OPERATING MOTOR VEHICLES WHEN
CERTAIN TYPE OF POLICY ISSUED
Section Sixteen . (a ) When a certificate is filed
showing that a policy or policies have been issued
covering all motor vehicles owned by the insured but
not insuring such person when operating any motor
vehicle not owned by him it shall be unlawful for
such person to operate any motor vehicle not owned
by him or not covered by such certificate . In such
event the Commissioner shall designate the above
restriction upon the operator's or chauffeur's license
of such person.

cil may make either treasurer or clerk of council
collector ; may make clerk collector of street im-
provement taxes . At any regular meeting of the
council they may appoint a collector , who shall hold
his office for two years from his appointment , and
until his successor is appointed and qualified . The
collector shall be over twenty-four years of age,
and a resident of the city for six months before his
appointment . Before he enters upon his duties he
shall take an oath to faithfully perform his duties
as collector , and execute bond , with security, to be
approved by the council , conditioned for the faith-
ful performance of his duties , and to pay over to
the person or persons entitled thereto all moneys
that may come into his hands as such collector . It
shall be the duty of said collector to collect all dues
owing to the city placed in his hands . The council
shall fix by ordinance , previous to his appointment ,
the compensation for his services . All dues owing
to the city may be collected by the chief of police .
The board of council , however , may by ordinance
provide that the treasurer shall perform the duties
of collector , or it may by ordinance provide that the
clerk of the council shall possess the qualifications
of collector and in such case may further provide
by ordinance that the clerk of the council shall per-
form the duties of collector . No demand shall be
necessary for the collection of said taxes , but the
same shall be payable at the office of the town treas-
urer , or at the office of the clerk of the council as
may by ordinance be provided .

Section Nineteen . This act shall be deemed to
create an additional and alternate method for the
acquisition of water works and/or sewerage system
by any city of the second , third , fourth , fifth and
sixth class , and shall not be deemed to include , alter ,
amend or repeal any other statute , except such as
conflict herewith . No proceeding shall be required
for the acquisition of any water works hereunder or
the acquisition of any sewerage system , or the is-
suance of bonds hereunder except such as are pre-
scribed by this Act , any provision in the General
Laws of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or charter
of any city to the contrary notwithstanding . All
the powers heretofore vested in cities of the second ,
third and fourth class relative to the acquisition of
water works and the issuance of bonds therefor , as
provided in Chapter one hundred thirty -three ( 133 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) , and as amend-
ed by Chapter one hundred nine ( 109 ) of the Acts
of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) ex-
tending the provisions of said original Act to the
acquisition of sewerage system in said cities are
hereby extended in full to cities of the fifth and
sixth class .
Whereas , many of the cities to which this Act is
applicable are now without adequate water works
or sewerage facilities , thus endangering the health ,
safety , and convenience of the inhabitants thereof ,
and whereas , some of the cities are unable to procure
loans from the Federal Government until this Act
becomes a law , therefore , an emergency is declared
to exist and this Act shall take effect , immediately
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .
Approved February 21, 1936 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
CONTINUANCE OF BOARD AND MANNER OF APPOINT-
MENT
One ( 1 ) . That the Kentucky State Board of
Dental Examiners heretofore created be continued ,
to consist of five practicing dentists , whose duty
it shall be to carry out the purposes and enforce the
provisions of this Act as hereafter specified . The
members of said board shall be appointed by the
Governor from a list of three names , for each mem-
ber to be appointed to be recommended by the Ken-
tucky State Dental Association , at its annual meet-
ing , and at the time of their appointment upon said
board must have been actual residents and legally
licensed practicing dentists of this State for a
period of four years or more immediately preced-
ing their appointment : Provided , however , That
no person shall be eligible to appointment to said
board who is in any way connected with or inter-
ested in any dental college or dental department of
any institution of learning or dental supply busi-
ness . The term for which the members of said
board shall hold office shall be four years , provided
the present members of the board in office at the
time of the passage of this Act shall continue in
office until their respective terms have expired and
until their successors are appointed and qualified .
All vacancies in said board shall be filled by the
Governor from said list .

classification.summary: This act continues the Kentucky State Board of Dental Examiners, specifying its composition, appointment process by the Governor from recommendations by the Kentucky State Dental Association, and qualifications for its members. classification.title: CHAPTER 89 283
classification.summary: This statute outlines requirements for registering a license with the county clerk within ninety days of issuance or upon removal to another county. Failure to register results in forfeiture of the license, which can be restored upon payment of a twenty-five dollar penalty. It also details examination fees for practicing dentistry, including a forty-dollar examination fee and a five-dollar fee for licenses and duplicate licenses. Board members receive ten dollars per diem for their duties and expenses, with the secretary receiving a salary. All expenses are to be paid from collected fees and penalties, not the State Treasury. Excess funds are held by the Secretary-Treasurer for the board's expenses.

classification.title: Renewal Certificates of License
classification.summary: This statute outlines the requirements for dentists to renew their licenses annually. It mandates the submission of signature, address, a fee of three dollars, and the registration certificate number to the Board of Dental Examiners by December 31st each year. It also specifies conditions for license renewal, including a requirement for active practice for the preceding five years. Failure to obtain a renewal certificate by March 1st results in cancellation, which can be restored upon payment of a twenty-five dollar fee within one year. The statute also addresses penalties for fraud in obtaining registration certificates, classifying it as a felony with imprisonment penalties.

DISPOSITION OF FEES
Seventeen ( 17 ) . All fees , imposed and collected
under the provisions of this Act , shall be paid to
the Secretary-Treasurer of the Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners for the use of said
Board .
DRUGGISTS MAY FILL DENTISTS ' PRESCRIPTIONS
Eighteen ( 18 ) . Legally licensed druggists of this
State may fill prescriptions of legally licensed
dentists of this State for any drug necessary to the
practice of dentistry . Dentists may sign death cer-
tificates the same as physicians , when necessary in
the line of their profession .
Nineteen ( 19 ) . This Act shall not prevent stu-
dents from performing dental operations under the
supervision of competent instructors within the
dental school , college , or dental department of a
university recognized by the Kentucky State Board
of Dental Examiners : Provided , the board at its
discretion may authorize the students of any dental
college , dental school , or dental department of a
university to practice dentistry , in any of the State
or municipal institutions , or public schools , or under
the board of health , or public clinic , or charitable
institution ; provided they are at all times under the
direct supervision of a licensed dentist in Kentucky ,
who is an instructor of the institution at which the
students are studying .
The provisions of this Act are severable , and if
any of its provisions shall be held unconstitutional
the decisions of courts shall not affect or impair
any of the remaining provisions of this Act . It is

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Any person , firm or corpora-
tion who shall , with actual or constructive notice of
a recorded real estate mortgage or vendor's lien ,
and without the written consent of the record holder
of same , purchase or sever or remove from the land
covered by said mortgage or lien , any standing tim-
ber , buildings , minerals , or improvements in the
nature of fixtures real , covered by and included in
said mortgage or lien, and who shall convert same
to his own use , shall take same subject to said mort-
gage or vendor's liens , and the respective priorities
thereof, and shall be liable to the holders of said
mortgages or liens for a return of same or for the
reasonable market value of said property at the
time of the purchase or severance or removal and
conversion thereof ( at the option of the holder of
said mortgage or lien ) , provided , however , that no
holder of a mortgage or a vendor's lien shall recover
more than the amount of the indebtedness secured
by his mortgage or vendor's lien ; and provided fur-
ther that any and all amounts recovered by the said
holder of said mortgage or vendor's lien shall be
credited on the indebtedness secured thereby .

classification.title: An Act relating to mortgages and vendor's liens and the removal of property subject thereto.
classification.summary: This act establishes liability for individuals or entities who, with notice of a recorded real estate mortgage or vendor's lien, remove or convert property (timber, buildings, minerals, fixtures) from the land without the written consent of the lienholder. The offender takes the property subject to the lien, and is liable for its return or its reasonable market value at the time of conversion, at the option of the lienholder. Recoveries are credited against the secured indebtedness.
classification.reasoning: The statute addresses the legal ramifications of removing property subject to a mortgage or vendor's lien without consent. It focuses on property rights and financial liabilities related to real estate transactions. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. Section Four ( 4 ) Because of the fact that a
great amount of existing indebtedness is secured by
mortgages and vendors ' liens on real estate situated
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and because
of the fact that the security of great numbers of said
mortgages and vendors ' liens is being endangered ,
impaired and destroyed in the manners above set
forth ; and because of the fact that the existing laws
do not afford adequate security to said holders of
said mortgages or vendors ' liens ; and because of the
fact that, for said reasons , the procurement of loans
or credit on such security is becoming difficult and
is being curtailed and terminated by certain large
and important lending agencies , both public and
private ; and because of the fact that the continuance
of the granting of loans and the advancement of
credit, based upon and secured by real estate mort-
gages and vendors ' liens , is necessary for the best
interests and general welfare of the people of Ken-
tucky -an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
and this Act shall become of full force and effect im-
mediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Section Five ( 5 ) . All Acts and parts of Acts ,
and all laws and parts of laws , in conflict herewith ,
are hereby repealed . Approved February 6, 1936 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That all assessments of prop-
erty heretofore made or hereafter made in territory
annexed thereafter to another civil division , prior to
such annexation shall be certified by the assessor
making the same , to the annexing civil division and
by the proper authority thereof , submitted to its
board of equalization to be equalized as provided by
law ; and when so equalized the regular annual tax
of the annexing division shall be levied by the proper
authority thereof and placed upon its tax bills made
therefor and collected as its other taxes ; and said
taxes if allowed to become delinquent , shall be sub-
ject to the same interest and penalty charged and
enforced in the manner provided by the law govern-
ing such annexing division .
Section Two . Should the equalization board
be about to adjourn when , or have adjourned before
said assessment is certified as provided in the pre-
ceding section , the time of such board may be ex-
tended for a reasonable time or it shall be recon-
vened for the purpose of passing upon such certified
assessment ; and should , for any reason , such assess-
ment and equalization be completed after the regu-
lar levy of taxes for that year have been levied , a
levy may thereafter be made as soon as practicable
for that year's tax .
Section Three . Whereas territories in this
State have been annexed at the last election , and the

the ensuing year because the levies are directed in

classification.title: An Act relating to the equalization of assessments and tax levies in annexed territories.
classification.summary: This act outlines the process for equalizing property assessments and tax levies when territory is annexed by a civil division. It details how prior assessments should be certified, equalized by the annexing authority's board of equalization, and how taxes should be levied and collected. It also provides for extensions of time for the equalization board if necessary and allows for a levy to be made after the regular tax period if equalization is completed late. The act states an emergency exists because territories annexed at the last election could otherwise not be taxed for the ensuing year.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . No State tax shall be levied , as-

ever , that nothing in this Act shall be construed as
releasing or prohibiting the collection of any real
estate taxes which may be due the State at the time
of the effective date of this Act.
Section Two . A tax at the rate of fifty cents
(50c ) upon each one hundred dollars ( $100.00 ) of
the value of all property which is directed by law
to be assessed for taxation except real estate , and
except as provided in paragraphs ( a ) and (b ) shall
be paid annually by the owner , person , or corpora-
tion assessed . ( a ) A tax at the rate of one -tenth of
one per cent shall be paid annually upon the amount
of deposits in any bank , trust company , or combined
bank and trust company , organized under the laws
of this State , or in any national bank of this State ,
as now provided by law ; and (b ) Building and Loan
Associations incorporated under the laws of this
State , and making loans to members only, shall be
assessed except as otherwise provided by law in the
manner and at the rate now set forth in Chapter
fourteen ( 14 ) of the Acts of the extraordinary ses-

CHAPTER 100 .
AN ACT authorizing fiscal courts in counties having one or
more cities of the second class to appropriate county funds
for the aid and benefit of " Dependent " children committed to
recognized associations , institutions , or corporations situated
in such counties , and which embraces in its purposes the
care , guardianship , and subsistence of children , and stipulat-
ing limitation of appropriation therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Appropriation by Fiscal Court .
That the fiscal court of each county of the State hav-
ing one or more cities of the second class may levy ,
collect and disburse such sums of money from the
general funds of its county as may be necessary to
comply with the provisions of this Act .
Section Two . The Dependent Children's Relief .
That each county having one or more cities of the
second class may contribute to the support of each
dependent child living within and under the care ,
guardianship , and subsistence of any recognized as-
sociation , institution , or corporation , being and lying
within such county , which embraces in its purposes
the care , guardianship , and subsistence of dependent
children, a sum not to exceed one hundred ( $ 100.00 )
dollars per annum to each such dependent child , said
sum to be known as " The Children's Relief " , and
payable quarterly in equal installments due in the
months , of February , May , August , and November ,
and on the first day thereof .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1936, p. 396

classification.summary: This act outlines the procedure for providing aid to dependent children. It requires that a child be registered as a ward of an association, institution, or corporation for at least ninety days prior to applying for benefits. The application and petition must be filed with the county clerk, who will then refer it to the county judge for an investigation into the child's eligibility as a 'Dependent Child' as defined by Carroll's Kentucky Statutes. If deemed eligible, the child will be committed to the applying entity and will receive the benefits provided by the act.

gency , in the fact that Water Districts have bonded
debts , some of which are now past due and in
default, due to the fact that a number of the prop-
erty holders are now delinquent and in default on
the payment of their assessments and the foreclos-
ure of said lien at this time would cause said prop-
erty holder to sacrifice his property , and due to the
further fact that there are no provisions made in
the said law to permit the refunding of said bond ,
therefore , this Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 291 .
AN ACT to add to the primary road system a certain road in
Carter and Lawrence counties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That a certain road be added to the primary road
system and more fully described as follows :
Beginning at Hitchins , Kentucky , in Carter Coun-
ty , thence to Denton , in Carter County , thence up
Straight Creek to Cunningham's store , thence down
East Fork by way of Glennwood , in Lawrence Coun-
ty, intersecting with the Mayo Trail at or near the
Riffe filling station .
This project is to be under the supervision and
control of the State Highway Commission and sub-
ject to all its laws thereof .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 377 .
AN ACT establishing as part of the system of State highways
a road known as the Coral Hill road in Barren County.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That there is hereby established
as a part of the primary system of State highways
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky a road in Bar-
ren County known as the Coral Hill road , said road
starting at its intersection with United States high
way thirty -one E ( 31E) at Foster's store , about
four miles north of Glasgow, and continuing
along its present route via Coral Hill school , via
Pleasant Union school , via Bradley's store , etc. , to
its intersection with the Hiseville - Sulphur Well road
at its present point near Halfway .
Section Two . That the construction , mainte-
nance , supervision and control of said road shall be
subject to and controlled by the provisions of Chap-
ter seventeen ( 17 ) of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of one thousand nine hundred twenty ( 1920 ) ,
and all other laws pertaining thereto establishing
and creating the primary system of State highways ,
to the same effect as if said road had been named in
the original Act as one of the projects of the pri-
mary system of State highways as provided therein.
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 408 .
RESOLUTION authorizing R. Young and Joseph Young to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission , or either .
WHEREAS on March twenty-third ( 23rd ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Joseph Young of Daviess County ,
Kentucky , was driving a wagon and team , the wagon being
loaded with coal , along and over highway sixty ( 60 ) immedi-
ately east of Owensboro , in Daviess County , Kentucky , and was
traveling east on his right hand side of the road , a portion
of his wagon and team traveling on the shoulder of the road
on his right . Said team of horses and wagon was the property
of R. Young, and Joseph Young was driving said team of
horses and wagon as the agent and servant of R. Young .
Traveling west on said highway sixty ( 60 ) at said time were
two trucks owned by the State Highway Commission and at
the time engaged in its service . One of said trucks was at-
tached to the rear of the other by a cable some ten or twelve
feet long , and while being driven west along said highway
by an employee of the State Highway Commission the rear
truck was by the carelessness and negligence of the driver
permitted to sway from side to side of the road , and by reason
of the carelessness and negligence of the employees of the
State Highway Commission in charge of said trucks , said
rear truck was permitted to swing with great force across
said highway striking said wagon and team driven by Joseph
Young in such a manner as to demolish said wagon , kill both
horses , throwing the driver , Joseph Young , from the wagon
to the pavement severely injuring and bruising him around
the head and body to such an extent that the injuries he
received are permanent . Said accident was caused and brought
about by the trucks being used and operated at said time by

structed a "Y" junction over a strip of land obtained from

tobacco owned by said R. L. Bayne and Dell Brown and
whereas by the construction of said junction , dust , dirt , lime
and other damaging substances were blown and washed over
said tobacco greatly damaging it to the great loss to said
Bayne and Brown , depriving the said Brown of his year's
work ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That R. L. Bayne and Dell Brown be and they
are hereby authorized and permitted to sue the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission in the Circuit Court of Shelby County , Ken-
tucky , for such damages as they may have sustained
by reason of the dirt and debris permitted to be
blown and thrown on their said tobacco .
No recovery shall be for a greater sum than seven
hundred and fifty dollars ( $750.00 ) .
No plea of limitation shall be interposed .
The Attorney General may investigate and com-
promise said claim if he deems advisable .

amount so adjudged or compromised shall be paid
by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn

CHAPTER 413 .
RESOLUTION for the relief of R. G. Holbrook , sheriff of Owen
County, Kentucky , for mileage and expenses incurred by him
as agent for the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , R. G. Holbrook on March sixteenth ( 16 ) , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirtfy -four ( 1934 ) , was duly appointed
by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as agent
of the Commonwealth to go to Greenville, North Carolina , to
present requisition papers of this Commonwealth to the Gov-
ernor of North Carolina and to bring back William Orville
Reed , a fugitive from the justice of this State , under indict-
ment for felony in Owen County , Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , said R. G. Holbrook , under said appointment , pro-
ceeded to the State of North Carolina and to the city of
Greenville to bring back said fugitive but found on arrival
there that said fugitive had escaped and that his whereabouts
were unknown , although he enlisted the aid of North Carolina
officers and made a thorough search for said fugitive , and
was compelled to return to Kentucky without him ; and
WHEREAS the said R. G. Holbrook , in performing his said
duty as agent aforesaid , incurred expenses and traveled one
thousand four hundred twenty -six ( 1,426 ) miles to and from
North Carolina and paid a fee to the Governor of North Caro-
lina for honoring said requisition , amounting in all to the
sum of one hundred and two dollars and fifty-six cents
($102.56 ) ; and

CHAPTER 415 565
WHEREAS , in the year one thousand nine hundred twenty-five
( 1925 ) , by condemnation proceedings , the Commonwealth of
Kentucky acquired , from M. E. Anderson and N. B. Anderson ,
in Carlisle County , Kentucky , a strip of land sixty feet in
width and about one mile in length , for the purpose of con-
structing a hard surface road thereon , which road is at pres-
ent known as State project No. 1 and Federal project No. 131 ,
and
WHEREAS , a stream of water known as Truman Creek flowed
under said highway, and
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission did begin the con-
struction of said highway where the present highway now
runs , from Bardwell , Kentucky , north to Wickliffe , Kentucky ,
in the year one thousand nine hundred twenty-six ( 1926 ) and
which highway was , by said Highway Commission , completed
in the year one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , and
WHEREAS , in the construction of said highway, the State
Highway Commission did cut some very deep barrow pits or
canals lengthwise with said highway and deeper than the
bed of Truman Creek and did divert the waters which had
formerly flown in Truman Creek from its original channel
and through said barrow pit for a distance of more than a
mile and which did cause the original channel of Truman
Creek to fill up with earth and sand by reason of the way the
barrow pits or canals were so cut or constructed , and
WHEREAS , the original channel of Truman Creek , on the east
side of said public highway and from the point where the
barrow pit or canal was cut from Truman Creek , lengthwise
with its highway, and which changed the water course so
that Truman Creek has not existed since , because it was filled
up with earth and sand for a distance of a mile and a half ,
and
WHEREAS in one thousand nine hundred twenty-eight (1928 ) ,
the State Highway Commission did plant a lot of growing
Willows in said barrow pit or canal , so constructed by it,
and did construct a wire fence in the bed and bottom of said
barrow pit or canal and that said Willows have grown in
great volume and , together with the fence construced therein ,
have caught new earth and sediment and have caused said
barrow pit or canal to fill up to such an extent that the
waters from Truman Creek will not flow therein but are
spreading over the lands of M. E. Anderson and N. B. Ander-
son and , yearly , since one thousand nine hundred twenty-

WHEREAS , a stream of water known as Truman Creek flowed
under said highway, and
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission did begin the con-
struction of said highway where the present highway now

WHEREAS , in the construction of said highway , the State High-
way Commission did cut some very deep barrow pits or canals
lengthwise with said highway and deeper than the bed of
Truman Creek and did divert the waters which had formerly
flown in Truman Creek from its original channel and through
said barrow pit for a distance of more than a mile and which
did cause the original channel of Truman Creek to fill up with
earth and sand by reason of the way the barrow pits or canals
were so cut or constructed , and
WHEREAS , the original channel of Truman Creek , on the east
side of said public highway and from the point where the
barrow pit or canal was cut from Truman Creek , lengthwise
with its highway, and which changed the water course so that
Truman Creek has not existed since , because it was filled up
with earth and sand for a distance of a mile and a half , and
WHEREAS , in one thousand nine hundred and twenty -eight

ing willows in said barrow pit or canal , so constructed by it ,
and did construct a wire fence in the bed and bottom of said
barrow pit or canal and that said willows have grown in great
volume and , together with the fence constructed therein , have
caught new earth and sediment and have caused said barrow

Section One ( 1 ) . WHEREAS in the fall of one thousand nine
hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , while driving an automobile on the

a truck which was owned at the time and being operated by
the employees of the State Highway Commission and was
severely , painfully and permanently injured on and about the
shoulder, arm and hand , and
WHEREAS it is claimed that said collision was entirely caused
and brought about by the gross negligence and gross careless-
ness of the agents , servants and employees of the said State
Highway Commission in the operation of said truck belonging
to the State of Kentucky , or the State Highway Commission ,
and
WHEREAS he sustained said serious , painful and permanent
injuries because of the alleged carelessness and negligence of
the said employees while in the discharge and conduct of the
State's business : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky
That said Charlie Coogle be, and he hereby is
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either in the Circuit Court of the county of his
residence , for such damages as he may have suf-
fered if any , by reason of any injuries received by
him through the carelessness or negligence of the
State Highway Commission , its agents or em-
ployees . Said suit shall be for any amount not ex-
ceeding the sum of two thousand five hundred dol-
lars ( $ 2,500.00 ) , and in the event any judgment is
recovered by said Charlie Coogle in said suit for
injuries to himself , or same is compromised or
settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public
Accounts by warrant drawn on the State Treasury
and paid out of the general fund .

CHAPTER 429 587
CHAPTER 428 .
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of copies of the Legis
lative Digest adopted at the session of one thousand nine
hundred and twelve , and every succeeding session since that
date , as the official publication for the General Assembly
and State and certain other public officials .
WHEREAS , It is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Senator and Representative be advised as to the various
and detailed steps of legislation ; and ,
WHEREAS , This General Assembly for the past seventeen ces-
sions has approved and adopted as its official publication
"The Legislative Digest ," a publication under the editorship
and management of competent and experienced persons , the
purpose of which is to fully advise from day to day the
officials and public of the Commonwealth as to matters affect-
ing them in an intelligent and prompt manner : Therefore,
Be it Resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring :
That the publishers of the Legislative Digest are
hereby directed to furnish daily a copy to each mem-
ber and the clerks of the General Assembly, and to
the heads of each State Department during the time
of this session . That the publishers are allowed the
sum of Ten Dollars for each subscription for the
Session and the Auditor of Public Accounts is
directed to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for
said amount when such service has been concluded .
The total number shall not exceed two hundred
(200 ) copies , which are to be paid for under this
resolution . Neither approved nor disapproved .

classification.title: An Act relating to Chapter 149 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1930
classification.summary: This act addresses penalties and limitations related to taxes imposed by a 1930 statute (Chapter 149). It states that payments made under that act were under compulsion due to severe penalties, waiving defenses based on failure to pay under protest. It also notes that Chapter 149 was initially held constitutional by the Kentucky Court of Appeals and a federal court, but later deemed unconstitutional by the U.S. Supreme Court. Consequently, the act waives the two-year limitation and other limitations imposed by the 1930 statute and Section 163 of the Kentucky Statutes.

WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission constructed that
part of State Highway No. seventy -one ( 71 ) within the cor-
porate limits of the city of Beaver Dam in the year one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) .

ployees did raise the levee eighteen (18 ) inches above the old

WHEREAS , insufficient openings were made in said levee to
carry the water accumulating above the said levee , causing

of the building of said D. G. Young , thereby causing the said

in the Ohio Circuit Court , and in case any judgment

tled and adjusted with the consent and approval of

lars ( $5,500.00 ) which was later withdrawn by the officials of

well & Company which was in excess of three hundred thou-

fund for the redemption of bonds and coupons , and as such
was inviolate , and ,
WHEREAS , the said Ohio County , by and through its agent ,
Gilmore Keown , has demanded a refund of both the State
Treasury and the State Highway Commission , and that the
State Treasury and Elam Huddleston has likewise demanded
refund to said Ohio County of thirty -four thousand seven
hundred twenty-five dollars and sixty -six cents ( $34,725.66 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Ohio County, Kentucky , be
and it is hereby , authorized and permitted to sue the
State Highway Commission and the Commonwealth
of Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court for such
interest as may be due on account of the premises
hereof and in the event of the recovery by said Ohio
County, Kentucky in said suit, or, upon the presenta-
tion of the facts and agreement of the parties or
their agents , said amount shall be paid by the State
of Kentucky by warrant on the Treasury but without
an execution being issued , but the judgment or pay-
ment shall , in no event exceed the sum of thirty -four
thousand seven hundred twenty-five dollars and six-
ty-six cents ( $ 34,725.66 ) , and legal interest thereon ,
from January 1 , one thousand nine hundred twenty-
eight ( 1928 ) , to date of payment , and in the event
of disagreement or exception to a judgment .
Section Two . Either party shall have the right
to appeal as in any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the

Section One . That Andy Hunter and Mathew
Hunter be and they are hereby empowered and au-

severally and respectively , against the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commis-
sion , or either, or both , for the purpose of determin-
ing the liability of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the State Highway Commission , or either, for
such injury and death , if any there be : and injury to
person or property , if any there be : and in the event
any judgment in either action is recovered by said
Andy Hunter or Mathew Hunter or their representa-
tives , or either of them , or same is compromised or
settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public
Accounts by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer
and paid out of the general fund .
Section Two . Either party to any such suit may
appeal from any judgment which may be entered
therein as in any other civil suit and the case may
be settled and adjusted with the consent and ap-
proval of the Attorney General of Kentucky , in the
same way as any other civil case .
Section Three . Said action may be brought in
any county of Kentucky , having jurisdiction of the
parties and subject matter.
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 466 635
Winston Elkins to Breathitt County to answer said indict-
ment , and whereas at the time it was impossible for him to
secure a requisition from the Governor of Kentucky , and that
he in compliance with the request of the officers of Breathitt
County, made the trip to Leavenworth , Kansas , and appre-
hended said Winston Elkins , and conveyed him back to
Breathitt County . Said Elkins was convicted and is now serv-
ing a three-year term in the State penitentiary at Frankfort ,
at the time the sheriff went after the prisoner the officials
were unable to contact the Governor to obtain a requisition ,
and that he immediately filed his claim for said expenses and
six cents (6c ) per mile for one thousand six hundred twenty-
six ( 1,626 ) miles , amounting to ninety -seven dollars and fifty-
six cents ($97.56 ) , and for gas and oil for car of ( $20.32 ) , and
for prisoners food one dollar forty cents ( $1.40 ) , making a
total of one hundred nineteen dollars and twenty -eight cents
( $119.28 ) , and that the Auditor of Public Accounts refused
to pay said claim on the grounds that he had not obtained a
requisition from the Governor of Kentucky : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed
to draw his warrant upon the treasurer for the sum
of one hundred nineteen dollars and twenty-eight
cents ($119.28 ) payable to Burnam Bach for his
services in the extradition of Winston Elkins from
Leavenworth , Kansas to Breathitt County, incurred
during the month of November , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
